azure.api.management_restapi_waf_acl_att.reason: Both rules target API Management service, enforce API access logging for security monitoring, and share the same resource type.

azure.app.service.deployment.slots.basic.reason: No config rule matches the same service (App Service), security property (basic auth), and resource type (deployment slots).

azure.storage.account.encryption.at.rest.reason: Both rules target Azure Storage account encryption at rest, ensuring same service, security property, and resource type.

azure.app.service.environment.minimum.tl.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure App Service Environment TLS version, while config rules target Service Bus and Storage Account resources.

azure.aks.cluster.private.endpoint.and.p.reason: Catalog rule targets AKS clusters while config rules target Kusto clusters - different resource types and services.

azure.function.app.function_restrict_pub.reason: Both rules target Azure Function Apps to restrict public network access, implementing the same security control for the same resource type.

azure.backup.recovery.services.vault.sof.reason: Same service (Azure Backup), same security property (soft delete enabled), same resource type (Recovery Services Vault).

azure.entra.user.group_membership:az.act.reason: Same service (Azure AD/Entra), same security property (membership review enabled), same resource type (group).

azure.backup.vault.cross.subscription.re.reason: Both rules target Azure Backup Vaults to disable cross-subscription restore, implementing the same CIS Azure control 5.1.6 for backup security.

azure.entra.policy.require_mfa_for_manag.reason: Same service (Azure AD/Entra) and resource type (tenant) but catalog rule requires MFA for management API while config rule enforces password complexity.

azure.app.service.plan.private.endpoint..reason: Catalog rule targets App Service Plan SKU compliance for private endpoints, while config rules target API Management and SignalR services for private endpoint configuration.

azure.container.instance.managed.identit.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Container Instances with managed identity least privilege, while config rules address SQL Managed Instances and user-assigned identities, differing in service, resource type, and security property.

azure.cosmos.db_tables_pitr_enabled.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Cosmos DB Table API (point-in-time recovery), while config rules target Azure Storage Tables (different service and security property).

azure.compute.instance.profile_attached.reason: Both rules enforce instance profile attachment for Azure compute resources with identity/access security controls.

azure.databricks.workspace.check.no.publ.reason: Config rules target different services (DDoS Protection, Kubernetes) and resource types, not Databricks Workspace public IP settings.

azure.cosmos.db_tables_kms_cmk_encryptio.reason: Catalog rule targets Cosmos DB Tables with KMS CMK encryption, while config rules target Data Factory pipelines and Data Lake Analytics workflows - different services and resource types.

azure.dataprotection.backup.vault.immuta.reason: Both rules target Azure Backup Vault immutability for data protection compliance

azure.loadbalancer.v2.waf.acl.attached.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Load Balancer v2 with WAF ACL, while config rules target CDN and Front Door services, differing in resource type and service.

azure.key.vault_cmk_rotation_enabled.reason: Both rules target Azure Key Vault keys, enforcing customer-managed key (CMK) rotation for the same security property.

azure.managementgroup.subscription.part_.reason: Both rules enforce subscription enrollment in management groups for governance and compliance.

azure.mysql.server.audit.log.connection..reason: Different services (MySQL vs. Kubernetes/SQL) and resource types, no match.

azure.monitor.activity.log.alert.service.reason: Catalog rule requires service health alerts while config rules check generic alert existence/configuration

azure.storage.account.smb.minimum.versio.reason: Catalog rule enforces SMB minimum version while config rules enforce TLS minimum version - different security properties and protocols.

azure.policy.recorder_all_regions_enable.reason: Catalog rule requires Azure Monitor Logs collection from all regions, while config rules check threat detection or Sentinel workspace enablement, which are different security controls.

iam.password.policy.reuse.24.reason: Both rules target the same Azure AD tenant password policy property for preventing password reuse, specifically the last 24 passwords.

iam.password.policy.minimum.length.14.reason: Both rules enforce a 14-character minimum password length for Azure AD tenant password policies, covering the same service, security property, and resource type.

azure.storage.file.share.soft.delete.ena.reason: Both target Azure Storage file shares and data protection, but snapshots differ from soft delete functionality.

azure.app.service.identity.check.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure App Service (azure.app.service) while config rules target API Management and SignalR services, representing different resource types and services.

azure.app.service.deployment.slot.http.v.reason: Both rules target App Service deployment slots with same HTTP version security property for CIS Azure compliance.

azure.aks.authorization.mode.check.reason: Both rules enforce RBAC authorization mode for AKS API server security on same resource type.

azure.app.service.vnet.integration.check.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure App Service VNet integration, while config rules target API Management services (different resource type).

azure.azure.network_vpc.flow_logs_enable.reason: Catalog rule requires VPC/network flow logs while config rules check firewall logging and data lake trigger logs - different services, security properties, and resource types.

azure.batch.account.diagnostics.settings.reason: No config rule matches the same service (Azure Batch), security property (diagnostics logs enabled), and resource type (Batch account).

azure.databricks.workspace.private.endpo.reason: Different service (Databricks vs. OpenAI/API Management) and resource type.

azure.cosmosdb.network.restriction.enabl.reason: No config rule matches Cosmos DB network restriction requirement for same service and resource type.

azure.container.instances_task_definitio.reason: No config rule matches the same service (Container Instances), security property (task definition logging), and resource type.

azure.compute.disk.encryption.cmk.check.reason: Both rules enforce disk encryption with customer-managed keys for Azure compute disks.

azure.dataprotection.backup.vault.cross..reason: Catalog rule requires cross-region restore enabled, while config rules address cross-subscription restore and encryption at rest - different security properties.

azure.defender.for_servers_enabled.reason: Catalog rule is for Azure Defender for Servers, while config rules are for MariaDB/MySQL managed identity, differing in service, security property, and resource type.

azure.disk.unattached.cmk.encryption.che.reason: Different resource types (disk vs. kusto/backup) and security properties.

azure.elastic.san.volume.group.encryptio.reason: No config rule matches the same service (Elastic SAN), security property (volume group encryption with CMK), and resource type.

azure.defender.storage.alerts.export.ena.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Defender for Storage alerts export, while config rules target general Azure Monitor metric and activity log alerts, differing in service, security property, and resource scope.

azure.function.app.vnet.integration.chec.reason: Different service (Function App vs Web App/Kusto) and different resource type despite similar VNet integration property.

azure.function.app.vnet.integration.rout.reason: Same security property (VNet integration) but different resource type (Function App vs. Web App).

azure.function.app.deployment.slot.http..reason: Both rules target Azure Function App deployment slots and enforce latest HTTP version security property.

azure.functionapp.tls.encryption.in.tran.reason: Config rules target different services (Backup Vault, SQL Server) and resource types than the catalog rule (Function App).

azure.function.app.deployment.slot.acces.reason: Catalog rule requires access restrictions (IP restrictions), while config rules check public network access (different security property).

azure.keyvault.secret_rotation_enabled.reason: Config rules target different services (Key Vault keys and Machine Learning) and different security properties (KMS encryption/rotation) than the catalog rule's secret rotation requirement.

azure.functionapp.https.only.check.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Function Apps, while config rules target Storage Accounts and Web Apps, differing in resource type.

azure.loadbalancer.deletion.protection.reason: Catalog rule targets load balancer deletion protection, while config rules target SQL server deletion protection; different resource types and services.

azure.monitor.policy.specific_days_enabl.reason: Catalog rule focuses on Azure Monitor policy for specific days, while config rules address WAF logging, differing in service, security property, and resource type.

azure.storage.account.secure_transport_p.reason: Catalog rule enforces secure transport (HTTPS), while config rules address SAS and key policies, which are different security properties.

azure.storage.private.endpoint.presence..reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Storage, while config rules target Azure OpenAI and API Management, indicating different resource types and services.

azure.network.virtual.network.ddos.prote.reason: Both rules enforce DDoS protection on Azure Virtual Networks, matching service, security property, and resource type.

azure.aks.rbac.check.cluster.admin.usage.reason: Catalog rule checks cluster admin usage while config rules check RBAC defaults and service accounts.

azure.synapse.cluster.automated_snapshot.reason: Different service (SQL Database vs Synapse) and different resource type despite similar snapshot security property.

azure.vm.instance.managed.by.ssm.reason: Catalog rule targets VM instance management via SSM, while config rules target SQL Managed Instance properties and SKU, differing in service, resource type, and security property.

azure.monitor.policy.assignment.reason: Both rules involve Azure Policy assignments for monitoring/compliance, but the catalog rule's specific ISO 27001 controls (A.5.7, A.8.16) are broader than the config rule's focus on config recorder enablement.

azure.app.service.slot.check.minimum.tls.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure App Service slots while config rules target Service Bus namespaces and Storage accounts - different resource types and services.

azure.aks.service.account.default.usage..reason: Both rules target AKS default service account usage, enforcing the same security property on the same resource type (managed cluster).

azure.blob.storage.soft.delete.enabled.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Blob Storage soft delete, while config rules are for Key Vault soft delete and storage bucket versioning, differing in service, resource type, and security property.

azure.defender.ensure_defender_for_conta.reason: Config rule enables Defender for Cloud generally but doesn't specifically target Defender for Containers configuration.

azure.defender.ensure_defender_for_datab.reason: Config rule covers Defender for Cloud enablement but not specifically Defender for Databases

azure.defender.ensure_defender_for_arm_i.reason: Both rules enforce enabling Azure Defender for ARM, targeting the same service (Defender for Cloud), security property (threat protection), and resource type (subscription/ARM).

azure.defender.ensure_defender_for_keyva.reason: Config rules target different services (general Defender for Cloud/SQL) while catalog rule specifically requires Defender for Key Vault.

azure.function.app.function_url_authenti.reason: Both rules enforce authentication for Azure Function Apps, targeting the same service, resource type, and security property.

azure.functionapp.deployment.slot.minimu.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Function App deployment slots, while config rules target Service Bus namespaces and Storage Accounts, differing in resource type and service.

azure.netappfiles.encryption.key.source..reason: Different Azure services: catalog rule targets NetApp Files encryption, config rules target Backup Vault and Kusto Cluster encryption.

azure.postgresql.flexible_server_allow_a.reason: Different service (PostgreSQL vs API Management/SignalR) and resource type.

azure.search.service.domains_access_cont.reason: No config rule matches Azure Search service domain access control requirement

azure.app.service.function.slot.https.on.reason: Both enforce HTTPS-only access for Azure Function App slots, matching service, security property, and resource type.

azure.storage.account.default.network.ac.reason: Both rules enforce network access restrictions on Azure Storage accounts by requiring default network access to be denied.

azure.storage.account.network.bypass.azu.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure Storage accounts for network bypass, while config rules target AI Services and Media Services, differing in service and resource type.

azure.mysql.flexible_server_ssl_connecti.reason: Both rules enforce SSL connections for MySQL database servers, addressing the same security property on the same resource type.

azure.network.nsg.flow.logs.to.log.analy.reason: Both rules enforce NSG flow logs to Log Analytics for network security monitoring on virtual networks.

azure.aks.clusters_created_with_private_.reason: Different Azure services (AKS vs HDInsight/Kusto) and resource types.

azure.defender.ensure_defender_for_azure.reason: Both rules enforce enabling Azure Defender for SQL on Azure SQL databases, targeting the same service and resource type.

azure.ad.user.role.check.disabled.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure AD user role assignment checks, while config rules address storage local users and AD inactive user thresholds, differing in service, security property, and resource type.

azure.app.service.virtual.network.integr.reason: Catalog rule targets Azure App Service virtual network integration, while config rules target SignalR and API Management services, differing in resource type and security property.

azure.batch.account.authentication.modes.reason: Both rules target Azure Batch account authentication modes, ensuring only secure authentication methods are enabled.

azure.backup.enabled_for_vms.reason: Catalog rule requires VM backup while config rules address Defender for Cloud and hotpatching for different security properties.

azure.cosmosdb.account.firewall_use_sele.reason: Different Azure services: catalog rule targets CosmosDB, config rules target Batch and Storage accounts.

azure.cosmosdb.autoscale_enabled.reason: No config rule matches Cosmos DB autoscale requirement; rules are for different services and resource types.

azure.databricks.workspace.diagnostic.lo.reason: Catalog rule is for Azure Databricks workspace diagnostic logging, while config rules are for Azure Container Instances, representing different services and resource types.
